label: Recommendation, ranking & personalization

node.label: Recommendation, ranking & personalization
node.definition: Recommender systems retrieve, rank, and diversify items for users or contexts using behavior, content, constraints, and feedback loops.
node.why_it_matters: This system is an application boundary where technical capability, evidence, safety controls, economics, and user workflow must succeed together.
examples: workflows combining Recommendation, ranking & personalization with Matrix factorization & topic models
workflows combining Recommendation, ranking & personalization with Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees
workflows combining Recommendation, ranking & personalization with Dense vector retrieval
limitations: End-to-end quality is bounded by the weakest data, model, tool, interface, policy, infrastructure, and human-oversight component. Its direct dependencies include Matrix factorization & topic models, Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees.

dependencies.rationale: Recommendation, ranking & personalization integrates Matrix factorization & topic models as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Recommendation, ranking & personalization integrates Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
In this atlas graph, Recommendation, ranking & personalization has an `applies` dependency on Dense vector retrieval with `common` strength.
Recommendation, ranking & personalization integrates Margin, ranking & metric objectives as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Recommendation, ranking & personalization integrates Linear models & generalized linear models as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Recommendation, ranking & personalization integrates k-NN & Naive Bayes as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Recommendation, ranking & personalization integrates k-means, mixture models & clustering as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
